sub_entries.definition: poem, written on (or destined to be written on) a lambaṅ; poem in kakawin-form. The prefix pa- apparently has a causative function (cf linambaṅakĕn).

The meaning seems to be that stones and cows look so similar that they are interchanged (mistaken one for the other; ahĕle-hĕli?) when put one beside the other (palayanan?). sub_entries.definition: what may be said or thought, what is intended to be said or conveyed, what is meant, meaning, intention; (kaliṅanya:) that means, that is to say, one would say, evidently. It is prob. a ka#noun to liṅ (cf harĕp -- kaharĕp). Kalīṅa unambiguously in Ad 148.6, and in: tan pakalīṅa, kinalīṅa. But kaliṅanya, kaliṅan ika, etc. can always be read kaliṅa nika, etc (except KS and RL, see below).

6.3: (h)ana liriṅ aṅĕpĕr muṅgw iṅ sor niṅ liraṅ alurus. Is there a second liriṅ which can ``aṅĕpĕr''? The bal. commentary for this place gives ``rwan iṅ jaliriṅ molah''. Under jaliriṅ KBW gives this commentary and ``siñjaṅ cĕpuk jaliriṅ, Mal 203'' (not found in my copy); GR: djĕliriṅ or tjĕliriṅ, name of a small kind of cricket, Prĕg. 36. There is a possibility that this here is analiriṅ, a nasalization of sa-liriṅ, which could then apply to the preceding kumbaṅnya maliwĕran: ``like a glance'', i.e. flitting past.

definition: pretext, pretence, excuse; using a pretext, pretending. Cf Skt leśa: a small part, particle, little bit; (in rhet.) a figure of speech in which a statement is made indirectly; a figure of speech in which what is usually considered as an advantage is represented as a disadvantage (and vice versa). The OJ meaning can probably be traced back to this figure of speech.
